content:
2026-01-18

4:45 wake. Dexcom looked good overnight - staying steady. Coffee and Isaiah 40 this morning.

## To Do
- [ ] Finalize segment outline for [[Show Planning - January 24 Episode]]
- [ ] Check in with CPE residents before rounds
- [ ] Confirm Deacon Mosley's booth list for [[Community Health Fair Planning]]
- [ ] Text Naomi back about Sunday dinner plans

## Notes
- Sandra mentioned a great quote from Brené Brown - add to [[Sermon & Meditation Resource List]]
- Mercy knocked over the rosemary pot. Again.

2026-01-12

Long day on the palliative floor. Held space for a family going through the hardest thing. Came home, watered the zinnias, and let myself feel it before cooking dinner.

## To Do
- [x] Radio prep Wed session - worked through January 24 outline
- [x] Confirm Rev. Angela Dawson for January 24 guest slot - confirmed
- [ ] Pull Thurman quotes for opening meditation

## Reflections
Howard Thurman: "Don't ask what the world needs. Ask what makes you come alive." That's what I'm sitting with tonight.

2026-01-17

Tuesday. Morning meeting with Dr. Vickers about the two new CPE residents starting in October. We need to adjust supervision schedule.

## To Do
- [x] Send updated on-call rotation to team
- [ ] Draft welcome letter for incoming CPE residents
- [ ] Update [[CPE Continuing Education Tracker]] with last week's workshop hours

## Notes
- On-call rotation: my overnight this week is Thursday into Friday
- Picking up groceries for Dorothy on Friday 4:30 PM - add Metamucil and Earl Grey to list

2026-01-16

Sunday. Church was good - Pastor Greer preached on Psalm 46. I led the pre-service meditation. Felt grounded.

Sunday dinner: pot roast, mac & cheese, cornbread. Naomi came over. We talked about her caseload at Memphis Youth Services - she's carrying a lot right now. Reminded her she can't pour from an empty vessel.

## Notes
- Next Sunday: remind congregation about health fair date
- Garden: black-eyed Susans coming in beautifully. Bird feeders need refilling.

2026-01-15

Saturday. Studio by 5:30. Show went well - topic was grief and community, had good listener call-ins. Sandra and I did brunch at Cafe Eclectic after. She's thinking of applying for department chair. I told her she'd be great.

## Show Notes
- Opening meditation: Lamentations 3:22-23
- Segment 2: listener question on supporting a grieving friend
- Closing: Lauren Daigle, "You Say"

## To Do
- [ ] Send TJ clip timestamps for the podcast edit
- [ ] Start outline for January 24 - see [[Show Planning - January 24 Episode]]

2026-01-11

Sunday. First Sunday of the month - tithe processed, Dorothy's med refills set, sponsor invoice review done (see [[Sponsor Invoice Review - January 2026]]).

## To Do
- [x] Tithe - $250 to Grace Community Church
- [x] Dorothy's med refills - called pharmacy
- [x] Review sponsor invoices
- [ ] Reach out to Covenant Insurance Group re: annual renewal

## Notes
- Mercy has a vet appointment coming up - schedule that
- [[Radio Anniversary Episode - March 2026]] planning should start this month

Show Planning - January 24 Episode

Air date: Saturday, January 24, 2026, 7:00-9:00 AM
Theme: "When the Well Runs Dry: Restorative Rest for Caregivers"

## Guest
- Rev. Angela Dawson, chaplain at Golden Heart Medical Center - confirmed for 7:30-8:00 segment
- Contact: angela.dawson@gmail.com / (901) 555-0623

## Segment Outline
1. Opening meditation (7:00-7:10) - Isaiah 40:31 "They shall mount up with wings as eagles"
2. Listener check-in / call-in line open (7:10-7:25)
3. Guest interview: Rev. Dawson on chaplain burnout and boundary-setting (7:30-8:00)
4. Sponsor break - Covenant Insurance Group, Mitchell's Flowers
5. Music reflection - Brandi Carlile, "The Story" (8:00-8:05)
6. Practical segment: 3 practices for restorative rest (8:05-8:30)
7. Closing prayer and announcements (8:45-9:00)

## Sponsor Notes
- Covenant Insurance Group: 60-sec spot, confirm copy by January 21
- Mitchell's Flowers: 30-sec spot, winter bouquet promo
- Bluff City Auto Care: standard 30-sec rotation

## Music Playlist (draft)
- Lauren Daigle, "Rescue"
- Chris Tomlin, "Good Good Father"
- Brandi Carlile, "The Story"
- Patsy Cline, "I Fall to Pieces" (closing credits)

## Open Items
- [ ] Pull Thurman quotes for opening
- [ ] Confirm Covenant Insurance copy
- [ ] Send pre-show brief to TJ Jackson by January 22

Guest Notes - Rev. Angela Dawson

Name: Rev. Angela Dawson
Role: Chaplain, Golden Heart Medical Center
Phone: (901) 555-0623
Email: angela.dawson@gmail.com

## Background
Angela and I went through our CPE residency at Regional One around the same time. She specializes in trauma-informed care and caregiver support. She has a gift for making difficult truths approachable - she'll be great on the show.

## Previous Appearances
- Morning Reflections, March 2026: "Lament as a Spiritual Practice"
- Morning Reflections, October 2025: "Walking with Families in Crisis"

## Topics for January 24
- Chaplain burnout: what the research says
- Boundaries as a form of love, not avoidance
- Her self-care practices: what actually works

## Interview Questions (draft)
1. What does "compassion fatigue" actually feel like from the inside?
2. What's the hardest boundary you've ever had to set at work?
3. What do you say to caregivers who feel guilty for resting?
4. What's your go-to restorative practice?

## Notes
- She prefers to see questions in advance - send by January 21
- No objection to being quoted on social media
- Possible future topic: grief ministry training for lay people

Community Health Fair Planning - Grace Community Church

Date: October 3, 2026 (Saturday)
Location: Grace Community Church fellowship hall and parking lot
Lead: Rebecca Price + Deacon Charles Mosley
Contact: charles.mosley@gmail.com

## Goals
- Free health screenings for congregation and South Memphis community
- Connect families with local health resources
- Normalize preventive care in our community

## Confirmed Booths / Services
- Blood pressure & glucose screening (partnering with Bluff City Community College nursing program - Sandra Coles coordinating)
- Vision screening (Cooper Eye Care, Dr. James Howard)
- Dental hygiene info
- Mental health resources table (Sandra + Memphis Counseling Network)
- Nutrition info table (Lisa Moore, nutritionist)
- Bluff City Medical Center Pastoral Care outreach table (Rebecca)

## Logistics
- Setup: 7:30 AM | Open to public: 9:00 AM-1:00 PM | Breakdown: 2:00 PM
- Volunteers needed: 12 (Deacon Mosley recruiting from congregation)
- Refreshments: light snacks, water, lemonade - no alcohol
- Announcement in Sunday bulletin starting the last two Sundays before the fair

## Open Items
- [ ] Confirm Sandra Coles and nursing students (need two weeks before the fair)
- [ ] Order event banner - Canva design, print at FedEx Midtown
- [ ] Draft church bulletin announcement
- [ ] Coordinate with Dr. Howard's office on screening supplies
- [ ] Send volunteer sign-up link to congregation via Pastor Greer

Fall Festival Emcee Notes - Grace Community Church

Date: October 31, 2026
Event: Grace Community Church Fall Festival & Trunk-or-Treat
Role: Emcee (Rebecca Price)

## Schedule (draft)
- 4:00 PM: Setup / sound check
- 5:00 PM: Festival opens, trunk-or-treat begins
- 5:15 PM: Rebecca opens with welcome and prayer
- 5:30 PM: Kids' costume parade
- 6:00 PM: Games and activities (Deacon Mosley coordinating)
- 6:30 PM: Pie auction fundraiser (Rebecca emceeing)
- 7:30 PM: Closing prayer and family photos

## Emcee Notes
- Keep energy light and warm - families with little kids, this is joy
- Work in a gentle devotional moment - maybe a harvest/gratitude theme
- Have Pastor Greer close in prayer
- Music: upbeat worship and family-friendly playlist (not scary)

## Costume
- Rebecca: something comfortable and warm - October evenings cool in Memphis
- Maybe a classic character with a spiritual touch (Esther? Ruth?)

## Open Items
- [ ] Confirm pie auction logistics with Deacon Mosley
- [ ] Get kids' game list from youth ministry
- [ ] Draft opening remarks and emcee script (start October 1)
- [ ] Coordinate sound system with church AV team

Greenline Walk Log

Goal: 3-4 walks per week, 2-3 miles each. Part of weight management plan with Lisa Moore.

| January 10 | 2.2 mi | Short - needed to get back for an early call |

| January 5 | 3.0 mi | Post-show Saturday - good way to decompress |

## Notes
- Down 12 lbs since starting with Lisa Moore - slow and steady
- Greenline: Wolf River Greenway near Cooper-Young is my go-to
- Saturday yoga (YouTube) is separate - not logged here

Garden Notes - Winter 2026

## What's Happening (January 2026)
- Zinnia bed: Cut back and mulched for the season - planning coral and yellow again come spring
- Black-eyed Susans: Dormant on the south side of the porch; crowns covered against the cold
- Lavender: Two plants holding through the cold; one struggling in the far corner - might need more sun next year
- Rosemary: Fully established and evergreen, Mercy keeps knocking over the pot on the steps
- Bird feeders: 2 feeders, mostly cardinals and sparrows; refill every 7-10 days in the cold

## To Do
- [ ] Plan to move struggling lavender to sunnier spot near fence when it warms
- [ ] Add another bird feeder - suet block for woodpeckers
- [ ] Sketch the spring planting plan this week
- [ ] Start seeds indoors by late February for spring color

## Notes
- Backyard is my reset button. Even 10 minutes out here after a hard day changes everything.
- The rosemary has gotten big enough to harvest for cooking - try in cornbread
- Neighbor asked about cuttings from the black-eyed Susans - say yes, share them in spring

Sermon & Meditation Resource List

Running list of passages, quotes, and resources for Morning Reflections and Grace Community Church meditations.

## Scripture Passages (frequently used)
- Isaiah 40:31 - "They shall mount up with wings as eagles" - renewal, endurance
- Lamentations 3:22-23 - "Great is thy faithfulness" - morning mercies
- Psalm 46:10 - "Be still and know" - centering
- John 10:10 - "I came that they may have life" - abundance vs. survival mode
- Romans 8:38-39 - "Nothing can separate us" - grief ministry
- Matthew 11:28-30 - "Come to me, all who are weary" - caregiver burnout

## Quotes - Theology / Devotional
- Howard Thurman: "Don't ask what the world needs. Ask what makes you come alive, and go do it. Because what the world needs is people who have come alive."
- Howard Thurman: "There is something in every one of you that waits and listens for the sound of the genuine in yourself."
- Henri Nouwen: "The friend who can be silent with us in a moment of despair or confusion, who can stay with us in an hour of grief and bereavement, who can tolerate not knowing, not curing, not healing - that is a friend who cares."
- Henri Nouwen: "Our ministry can only be a true ministry when we ourselves are being ministered to."
- Brené Brown: "Vulnerability is the birthplace of love, belonging, joy, courage, empathy, and creativity."

## Books - Current Reading / Show References
- Henri Nouwen, *The Wounded Healer* - see [[Book Notes - The Wounded Healer]]
- Howard Thurman, *Jesus and the Disinherited*
- Brené Brown, *Atlas of the Heart*
- Barbara Kingsolver, *Unsheltered*
- Anne Tyler, *Redhead by the Side of the Road*

## Podcasts / Media
- On Being with Krista Tippett - regular source for guest topic inspiration
- Faith and Practice Journal - quarterly print subscription
- Sojourners - online articles for show prep

Book Notes - The Wounded Healer
Author: Henri Nouwen
Started: November 2025 | Finished: December 2025

## Core Thesis
The minister is called to recognize suffering in their own heart and to make that recognition the starting point of service. The wound becomes the source of healing - not despite its presence, but because of it.

## Key Ideas
1. Wounded healer archetype - The one who heals is also wounded. Pretending otherwise isolates both caregiver and care-receiver.
2. Hospitality as ministry - Creating space where a stranger can become a friend, where fear can be converted into trust.
3. Loneliness and solitude - Loneliness is the disease; solitude is the cure. A minister who cannot be alone cannot minister to others' loneliness.
4. The contemplative critic - Nouwen calls ministers to be present to suffering without being consumed by it.

## Quotes
- "The great illusion of leadership is to think that man can be led out of the desert by someone who has never been there."
- "We can only love because we are born out of love... Healing means, first of all, the full realization that we cannot be led out of the desert by someone who has never been there."

## Application to My Work
This book found me at the right time. When I read about the contemplative critic, I thought about the tension I carry between the hospital floor and the radio studio - both call for full presence, and full presence is exhausting. Nouwen gave me language for why rest is not retreat but renewal.

## Use in Show
- March 2026 episode: referenced hospitality chapter
- Possible future segment: "The Chaplain's Own Wound" - personal reflection on 15+ years in pastoral care

- ask TJ about archival clips for anniversary episode
